perturbation.original_text: Pinetree must protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature, and Pinetree must, at minimum, use at least reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree should endeavor to protect the Proprietary Information from unauthorized use or disclosure by exercising a degree of care it deems adequate, and Pinetree must, at minimum, use what it considers reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'must protect' to 'should endeavor' and replacing 'the same degree of care that Pinetree uses with respect to information of its own of a similar, highly valuable nature' with 'a degree of care it deems adequate' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation. 'Reasonable care' is also replaced with 'what it considers reasonable care' adding to the ambiguity, making the protection level subjective. This creates an ambiguous obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: The MUTSA requires reasonable efforts to maintain the secrecy of trade secrets. By allowing Pinetree to use a degree of care 'it deems adequate' and 'what it considers reasonable care,' the modified text contradicts the objective standard of 'reasonable efforts' required under Maryland law, potentially leading to insufficient protection of trade secrets and making enforcement difficult.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203 (2022) - Right to Damages :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1203 - Right to Damages Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1203 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next (a) Except to the extent that a material and prejudicial change of position prior to acquiring knowledge or reason to know of misappropriation renders a monetary recovery inequitable, a complainant is entitled to recover damages for misappropriation. (b) Damages under this subtitle may include: (1) The actual loss caused by misappropriation; and (2) The unjust enrichment caused by misappropriation that is not taken into account in computing actual loss. (c) In lieu of damages measured by any other methods, the damages caused by misappropriation may be measured by imposition of liability for a reasonable royalty for a misappropriator’s unauthorized disclosure or use of a trade secret. (d) If willful and malicious misappropriation exists, the court may award exemplary damages in an amount not exceeding twice any award made under subsection (a) of this section. Previous Next Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. Maryland may have more current or accurate information. perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity regarding the standard of care required to protect proprietary information. The original text states Pinetree 'must protect' using 'the same degree of care' as it uses for its own highly valuable information and 'at minimum, use at least reasonable care.' The changed text weakens this obligation by using 'should endeavor to protect' and allowing Pinetree to use 'a degree of care it deems adequate' and 'what it considers reasonable care.' This shift to subjective standards contradicts the reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy required to protect trade secrets under the MUTSA, as indicated in the Law Explanation. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not helpful, Scraped Law Snippet 2, which references Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1203 regarding damages for misappropriation, indirectly supports the argument that a failure to adequately protect trade secrets can lead to legal consequences, implying a standard of care is required, even if not explicitly stated in this snippet. The change creates a legal contradiction by creating ambiguity around the duty of care, creating an ambiguous legal obligation and making the contract unenforceable, as this is in line with the definition of the Ambiguities perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Pinetree shall not copy, duplicate, replicate, decompile or reverse engineer in any manner whatsoever (whether physically, electronically, in writing or otherwise), in whole or in part, any part of the Proprietary Information without the prior written consent of STI, which consent shall be in STI's sole and absolute discretion, and will immediately notify STI in writing of any unauthorized disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree shall refrain from substantially copying, duplicating, replicating, decompiling or reverse engineering in any material manner any significant part of the Proprietary Information without STI's general approval, and will endeavor to promptly notify STI of any potential unauthorized disclosure of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not copy' to 'shall refrain from substantially copying' introduces ambiguity about what constitutes 'substantial copying.' Replacing 'in any manner whatsoever' with 'in any material manner' and 'any part' with 'any significant part' further weakens the restriction. Replacing 'prior written consent of STI, which consent shall be in STI's sole and absolute discretion' with 'STI's general approval' reduces STI's control, and changing 'will immediately notify STI' to 'will endeavor to promptly notify STI' makes the notification less certain. This creates an ambiguous obligation.
perturbation.law_explanation: The MUTSA defines misappropriation to include acquisition of a trade secret by improper means or disclosure or use of a trade secret without consent by a person who knew or had reason to know that his knowledge of the trade secret was derived from a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief. The changes make it difficult to prove misappropriation, as 'substantial copying', 'material manner' and 'significant part' are open to interpretation. This undermines the protection afforded by the MUTSA.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1201 (2022) - Definitions :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1201 - Definitions Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1201 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Next (a) In this subtitle the following words have the meanings indicated. (b) “Improper means” includes theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. (c) “Misappropriation” means the: (1) Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or (2) Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: (i) Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or (ii) At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that the person’s knowledge of the trade secret was: 1. Derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; 2. Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or 3. Derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (iii) Before a material change of the person’s position, knew or had reason to know that it was a trade secret and that knowledge of it had been acquired by accident or mistake. (d) “Person” means an individual, corporation, business trust, statutory trust, estate, trust, partnership, association, joint venture, government, governmental subdivision or a
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity around key terms like 'copying,' 'manner,' and 'part,' and weakens the requirement for immediate notification. This ambiguity makes it difficult to prove misappropriation of trade secrets, as the terms 'substantial copying,' 'material manner,' and 'significant part' are open to interpretation, undermining the protection afforded by the MUTSA, as stated in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 2 defines 'misappropriation' as including the acquisition of a trade secret by improper means or disclosure or use of a trade secret without consent. The changes to the contract language weaken the ability to enforce the trade secret protection, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Pinetree agrees to do such further acts and to execute and deliver such additional agreements and instruments from time to time as STI may at any time reasonably request in order to assure and confirm unto STI its rights, powers and remedies conferred in the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Pinetree agrees to consider performing such further acts and executing and delivering such additional agreements and instruments from time to time as STI may request to generally support STI's rights, powers, and remedies under the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'agrees to do' to 'agrees to consider performing' weakens the obligation, making Pinetree's compliance discretionary. Replacing 'reasonably request' with 'may request' removes the reasonableness constraint, but more importantly, replacing 'in order to assure and confirm unto STI its rights, powers and remedies conferred in the Agreement' with 'to generally support STI's rights, powers, and remedies under the Agreement' makes the purpose of the further acts vague. This creates an ambiguous obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Maryland Contract Law - Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: While Maryland doesn't have a specific statute codifying the duty of good faith and fair dealing in contract law, Maryland courts imply a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. This covenant requires each party to act honestly and not frustrate the other party's right to receive the fruits of the contract. By making Pinetree's agreement to perform further acts discretionary and only for 'general support,' the modified text allows Pinetree to potentially undermine STI's rights under the agreement, contradicting the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within the Uniform Commercial Code imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement. Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing. By changing 'agrees to do' to 'agrees to consider performing', Pinetree's obligation becomes discretionary, potentially undermining STI's rights. Additionally, changing the purpose to 'generally support' introduces ambiguity. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines good faith as encompassing 'honest dealing' and 'faithful performance of duties'. The modified text allows for a less than faithful performance, and thus contradicts the duty of good faith.
